lines.en_lit: that they are arranged upon the altar, and if cannot hold them, that they are arranged upon the ramp or upon the ledge until the large arrangement is made and they are arranged The verse states: “That which the fire will consume of the burnt-offering, on the altar”

And Rabbi Meir parts of a burnt-offering consumed you should return them but you do not return incense consumed As Rabbi Ḥananya bar Minyomi from the school of Rabbi Eliezer ben Ya’akov taught “That which the fire will consume of the burnt-offering on the altar” parts of a burnt-offering consumed you should return them; but you do not return incense consumed
At any rate, everyone that they add on that day, From where they They derive it “And the fire And even one who does not expound “and” does expound “and the.”

“a perpetual fire” for what come It is required for that was taught “A perpetual fire shall be kept burning on the altar, it shall not go out” teaches about the second arrangement for incense, that it is only on the outer altar.

From where fire a coal pan and for the candelabrum It is a logical derivation. “Fire” is stated with regard to the incense and “fire” is stated with regard to the coal pan and the candelabrum. Just as there, upon the outer altar, so too upon the outer altar.

Or, go this way. “Fire” is stated with regard to the incense and “fire” is stated with regard to the coal pan and the candelabrum. Just as there, near to it, so too, coal pan and the candelabrum, near to it,
The verse states: “A perpetual fire shall be kept burning on the altar, it shall not go out” “perpetual fire” that I told you, should only upon the top of the outer altar.

We learned fire for candelabrum. From where fire for the coal pan It is a logical derivation. “Fire” is stated with regard to coal pan and “fire” is stated with regard to candelabrum. Just as there, upon the outer altar, so too, here, upon the outer altar.
Or, go this way: “Fire” is stated with regard to the incense and “fire” is stated with regard to the coal pan Just as there, near to it, so too, here, near to it,

The verse states: “And he shall take a pan full of coals of fire from upon the altar from before the Lord” Which altar partially before the Lord, but not all of it is before the Lord, You must say this is the outer altar.

And it is necessary to write “from upon the altar” and it is necessary to write “from before God” because if the Merciful One had written “from upon the altar” I would have said: What altar inner altar. the Merciful One writes “from before God.” And if the Merciful One had written “from before God,” I would have said specifically from before God,
